creator: chris@aims.com.au
text:
Howdy,

> From: Dirk Froemberg <dirk@freebsd.org>
> To: freebsd-gnats-submit@freebsd.org
> Subject: Re: ports/30028: Issue between www/mod_php4 and graphics/gd
> Date: Mon, 27 Aug 2001 13:52:30 +0200
>
>  Hi Chris!
>
> On Mon, Aug 27, 2001 at 06:09:48PM +1000, Chris Knight wrote:
> > Well, it compiles now, but the GIF support isn't built correctly. This
> > doesn't phase me as I only need GD for PNG support.
> > BTW, line 9 of php_configure.3.diff should read:
> >
> > #ifdef HAVE_GD_GIF_READ
>
> % cat -n php_configure.3.diff | grep '^ *9	'
>      9	+#ifdef HAVE_GD_GIF_READ
>
> Can't see any difference... ;-)
>
> Or are you refering to line 12?
>     12	+#ifdef(HAVE_GD_GID_CREATE)
>
> This should read HAVE_GD_GIF_CREATE. But since HAVE_GD_GIF_CREATE
> isn't defined for our libgd anyway it shouldn't matter.
>
Yes, I was referring to line 12 - had to go for a bit of a dig to confirm
this! That will teach me for being tardy, I guess.
I'm happy for this PR to be closed. It was the build issue at the time that
was the problem. This has since been resolved. The issue of GD functionality
and library versioning was also an issue, but again this has also been
resolved.
Thanks

> 	Regards Dirk
>
> --
> Dirk Froemberg

Regards,
Chris Knight
Systems Administrator
AIMS Independent Computer Professionals
